name: Google Search
summary: Search Google (web/news/books) and extract organic results.
description: Search Google's web, news, or books vertical and extract title/url/snippet for each organic result.

search_query.description: Query text. The engine accepts the aliases query and q as well.

vertical.description: Which Google vertical to search. Defaults to web. news → tbm=nws, books → tbm=bks.

steps.name: Open Google homepage (creates tab for the SPA redirect in s2)
Wait for at least one result card (web/news/books)
Extract organic results (news via .WCv1we; web/books via .MjjYud + h3)

help.summary: Search Google (web/news/books) and extract organic results.

parameters.search_query: Query text. The engine accepts the aliases query and q as well.
parameters.vertical: Which Google vertical to search. Defaults to web. news → tbm=nws, books → tbm=bks.

examples.command: rzn-browser run google search --param search_query="rust browser automation"
rzn-browser run google search --param query="AI regulation" --param vertical=news
examples.description: News vertical (uses the query alias). Returns extra source + age fields per result.

examples.command: rzn-browser run google search --param search_query="category theory" --param vertical=books
examples.description: Books vertical (titles + URLs; snippets often empty).

returns: Array of { title, url, snippet, source?, age? }. source/age populate for news results only; snippet is often empty for books cards.
notes: Shopping is intentionally NOT included — Google moved shopping to a React widget at udm=28 with no clean per-product anchors. A dedicated google/shopping workflow would be needed; out of scope here.
For raw image results, use google/images — different output contract (image URLs).
For Scholar, use google/scholar — paper-shaped fields.

News uses .WCv1we cards; source/age are unclassed <span> nodes inside the card. We detect age by regex (e.g. '18 hours ago', '1 day ago', 'May 12, 2026'); source is the first short non-age span. Web/books cards keep source=age=null.
If results are empty, dismiss_popups may have failed on a consent gate — check the browser tab.
Unknown vertical values fall back to web.
